text: Agile methodology is a popular approach to managing projects, especially in software development. Instead of planning everything at once, teams work in short cycles called sprints, typically lasting two to four weeks. Each sprint begins with a planning meeting where the team selects tasks from a list called a backlog. These tasks are often written as user stories—simple descriptions of a feature from the user's perspective. Every day, the team holds a short standup meeting to discuss progress and any problems. This iterative development allows the team to adapt quickly to changes and deliver working parts of the product regularly.
